prompts.description: Activity step: hide CTA until selection, reveal with animation
prompts.prompt:
In `components/onboarding.tsx`, modify the Activity step (around line 1607) to hide the 'Continue' CTA until the user has selected an activity level, then reveal it with an animation. Find this block: ```jsx
Continue with this activity
``` Replace it with: ```jsx
Continue â€” {activityLevels.find(l => l.id === selectedActivityLevel)?.label} â†’
``` Do NOT change anything else in the activity step. Do NOT change the activityOptionButtonClass function or how activity cards render. Verify: `npm run build` passes with no TypeScript errors.

prompts.description: Goal step: hide CTA until selection, reveal with animation and contextual label
prompts.prompt:
In `components/onboarding.tsx`, modify the Goal step (around line 1683) to hide the 'Continue' CTA until the user has selected a goal, then reveal it with an animation. Find this block: ```jsx
Continue with this goal
``` Replace it with: ```jsx
Continue â€” {goals.find(g => g.id === selectedGoal)?.label} â†’
``` Do NOT change the goal card rendering or goalOptionButtonClass. Do NOT change any other step. Verify: `npm run build` passes with no TypeScript errors.

prompts.description: Diet, ingredient-mode, and quick-estimate body type: hide CTA until selection
prompts.prompt:
In `components/onboarding.tsx`, apply the same hide-until-selected CTA reveal pattern to three more steps. **1. Diet step** (around line 2002): Find: ```jsx
Continue with this style
``` Replace with a wrapper div using `overflow-hidden transition-all duration-200` and `max-h-[72px] opacity-100` when `!!selectedDietType`, `max-h-0 opacity-0` otherwise. Remove disabled prop. Update CTA label to: `Continue â€” {dietTypes.find(d => d.id === selectedDietType)?.label} â†’`. CTA className is always `bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/90`. Do NOT touch the macroPreview section above the CTA. **2. Ingredient-mode step** (around line 2103): Find the `
` containing the OnboardingPrimaryCta with `disabled={!ingredientMode}`. Wrap it in an outer div with `overflow-hidden transition-all duration-200` and `max-h-[72px] opacity-100` when `!!ingredientMode`, `max-h-0 opacity-0` when `!ingredientMode`. Remove the disabled prop and the `bg-muted` fallback className. Keep the existing contextual label logic (it already varies by ingredientMode â€” keep as-is). **3. Quick-estimate body type** (around line 1510, inside `step === 'quick-estimate' && entryChosen`): Find the `
` containing the OnboardingPrimaryCta 'Set my targets' with `disabled={!weight || !quickHeight || !quickAge || !quickBodyType}`. Wrap it in an outer div with `overflow-hidden transition-all duration-200` and `max-h-[72px] opacity-100` when `!!(weight && quickHeight && quickAge && quickBodyType)`, `max-h-0 opacity-0` otherwise. Remove the disabled prop and muted fallback className. Keep label 'Set my targets'. Keep the paragraph below ('You can refine this later...') and the 'Use detailed body stats' link below OUTSIDE this wrapper â€” they should always be visible. Do NOT change card rendering, option button classes, or other steps. Verify: `npm run build` passes with no TypeScript errors.
